ucs.title: [LLM] APT10 / Stone Panda DLL side-loading via legitimate signed binary in non-standard path (PlugX / ANEL / UPPERCUT loader)
ucs.description: Hunts APT10's signature delivery technique: dropping a legitimately-signed Microsoft / Java / McAfee / Google binary into a writable directory (ProgramData, AppData, Public) alongside a maliciously-replaced sibling DLL (jli.dll, msvcr100.dll, libcurl.dll, oci.dll, etc.) so the binary side-loads attacker code into a trusted process. This staging pattern is documented across PlugX, ANEL, ChChes, RedLeaves and UPPERCUT campaigns attributed to MenuPass / Stone Panda / Bronze Riverside.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("jjs.exe","javaw.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","DPMonitor.exe","FlashHelp_Player.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","vmnat.exe","hkcmd.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Intel\\*") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_hash Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process_path "(?<loader_dir>.+)\\\\[^\\\\]+$" | join type=inner dest loader_dir [ | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("jli.dll","msvcr100.dll","mscoree.dll","libcurl.dll","oci.dll","secur32.dll","cryptbase.dll","version.dll","wtsapi32.dll","netapi32.dll","goopdate.dll","FlashHelp.dll","PotPlayer.dll","vmtools.dll") Filesystem.action=created by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rex field=file_path "(?<loader_dir>.+)\\\\[^\\\\]+$" ] | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path process_hash parent_process_name file_name file_hash | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// APT10 / Stone Panda — legit binary in writable dir side-loading a malicious sibling DLL
let StagingDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Intel\"]);
let SideLoaders = dynamic(["jjs.exe","javaw.exe","java.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","DPMonitor.exe","FlashHelp_Player.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","vmnat.exe","hkcmd.exe","avp.exe","OInfoP11.exe"]);
let APT10Dlls = dynamic(["jli.dll","msvcr100.dll","mscoree.dll","libcurl.dll","oci.dll","secur32.dll","cryptbase.dll","version.dll","wtsapi32.dll","netapi32.dll","goopdate.dll","FlashHelp.dll","PotPlayer.dll","vmtools.dll"]);
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ (APT10Dlls)
| where FolderPath has_any (StagingDirs)                            // DLL dropped into writable staging dir
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SideLoaders)                 // loader is a known side-load target
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Windows\"     // not the genuine system copy
   and InitiatingProcessFolderPath !startswith @"C:\Program Files"
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath) == tolower(FolderPath) // adjacent — classic side-load layout
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Loader     = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderHash = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          LoaderCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DLL        = FileName,
          DllPath    = FolderPath,
          DllHash    = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT10 / Stone Panda — legit signed binary in writable dir side-loading malicious sibling DLL (Sysmon EID 7 image-load)
let StagingDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\Local\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Intel\"]);
let SideLoaders = dynamic(["jjs.exe","javaw.exe","java.exe","GoogleUpdate.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","mcods.exe","DPMonitor.exe","FlashHelp_Player.exe","PotPlayerMini.exe","vmnat.exe","hkcmd.exe"]);
let APT10Dlls = dynamic(["jli.dll","msvcr100.dll","mscoree.dll","libcurl.dll","oci.dll","secur32.dll","cryptbase.dll","version.dll","wtsapi32.dll","netapi32.dll","goopdate.dll","FlashHelp.dll","PotPlayer.dll","vmtools.dll"]);
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7
| extend Image       = tostring(EventData.Image),
         ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
         User        = tostring(EventData.User),
         Signed      = tostring(EventData.Signed),
         Hashes      = tostring(EventData.Hashes)
| extend LoaderName = tolower(extract(@"\\([^\\]+)$", 1, Image)),
         DllName    = tolower(extract(@"\\([^\\]+)$", 1, ImageLoaded)),
         LoaderDir  = tolower(extract(@"^(.+)\\[^\\]+$", 1, Image)),
         DllDir     = tolower(extract(@"^(.+)\\[^\\]+$", 1, ImageLoaded))
| where DllName in~ (APT10Dlls)
| where LoaderName in~ (SideLoaders)
| where ImageLoaded has_any (StagingDirs)
| where not(Image startswith @"C:\Windows\") and not(Image startswith @"C:\Program Files")
| where LoaderDir == DllDir
| where User !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, User, LoaderName, Image, DllName, ImageLoaded, Signed, Hashes
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT10 Stone Panda DLL Side-Loading via Legitimate Binary in Writable Path
id: a1c47e5e-7f2c-4d8b-9c1a-1f9c3a23d101
status: experimental
description: Detects APT10 / MenuPass / Stone Panda DLL side-loading where a legitimately-signed binary (Java jjs/javaw, GoogleUpdate, McAfee mcoemcpy, FlashHelp_Player, PotPlayer) executes from a non-standard writable path (ProgramData, AppData, Public, PerfLogs, Temp) and loads a sibling DLL with a name historically abused by ANEL, UPPERCUT, ChChes, RedLeaves and PlugX (jli.dll, msvcr100.dll, libcurl.dll, oci.dll, etc.).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0045/
  - https://www.pwc.co.uk/cyber-security/assets/cyber-threats-2019-retrospect.pdf
  - https://www.fireeye.com/blog/threat-research/2018/09/apt10-targeting-japanese-corporations-using-updated-ttps.html
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - detection.emerging_threats
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_loader_name:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\jjs.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\mcods.exe'
      - '\DPMonitor.exe'
      - '\FlashHelp_Player.exe'
      - '\PotPlayerMini.exe'
      - '\vmnat.exe'
      - '\hkcmd.exe'
  selection_loader_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\AppData\Local\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Intel\'
  selection_dll:
    ImageLoaded|endswith:
      - '\jli.dll'
      - '\msvcr100.dll'
      - '\mscoree.dll'
      - '\libcurl.dll'
      - '\oci.dll'
      - '\secur32.dll'
      - '\cryptbase.dll'
      - '\version.dll'
      - '\wtsapi32.dll'
      - '\netapi32.dll'
      - '\goopdate.dll'
      - '\FlashHelp.dll'
      - '\PotPlayer.dll'
      - '\vmtools.dll'
  filter_system_paths:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\'
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter_system_paths
fields:
  - Image
  - ImageLoaded
  - User
  - Hashes
  - Signed
falsepositives:
  - Portable application installers running out of staging directories
  - Java JDK installations placed in non-default user directories
level: high
ucs.rationale: Generic side-load detections fire on portable apps. This is locked to APT10 by intersecting THREE actor-specific facets: (1) the exact set of legit binaries Stone Panda has been observed re-purposing across the Cloud Hopper, A41APT and Operation Cuckoobees clusters (Java jjs/javaw for ANEL, mcoemcpy for ChChes/RedLeaves, FlashHelp_Player for UPPERCUT, GoogleUpdate.exe for PlugX), (2) APT10's docum

ucs.title: [LLM] APT10 / Cloudhopper trusted-relationship pivot — RDP from MSP / external host followed by schtasks persistence
ucs.description: Hunts the APT10 'Cloud Hopper' lateral-movement signature: an interactive RDP logon arriving on an internal host (often originating from an MSP-owned subnet, a jumpbox or a non-corporate IP) followed within 30 minutes by the same account spawning schtasks.exe /create to register a persistence task pointing at a loader in a writable directory (ProgramData / AppData / Public) — frequently masquerading as a Google/Windows updater. This RDP-then-schtasks chain is documented in PwC, BAE Systems and US-CERT TA17-117A on Cloud Hopper / Operation Cloud Hopper.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as logon_time from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success (Authentication.Logon_Type=10 OR Authentication.authentication_method="RemoteInteractive" OR Authentication.signature_id=4624) by Authentication.dest Authentication.user Authentication.src _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)` | where NOT match(user, "\$$") | rename _time as logon_time | join type=inner dest user [ | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" (Processes.process="*/create*" OR Processes.process="*-create*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename _time as task_time ] | eval delay_sec = task_time - logon_time | where delay_sec >= 0 AND delay_sec <= 1800 | rex field=process "(?i)/tr\s+[\"']?(?<task_target>[^\"']+)" | where match(task_target, "(?i)\\\\(ProgramData|AppData|Users\\\\Public|PerfLogs|Temp)\\\\") OR match(task_target, "(?i)(powershell|rundll32|regsvr32|wscript|cscript|mshta|certutil|bitsadmin)") | rex field=process "(?i)/tn\s+[\"']?(?<task_name>[^\"']+)" | where match(task_name, "(?i)(google|update|chrome|adobe|windows|defender|onedrive|java)") OR match(task_target, "(?i)\\.(exe|dll|bat|vbs|ps1|js)$") | table logon_time task_time delay_sec dest user src task_name task_target process parent_process_name | sort - task_time
ucs.kql:
// APT10 / Cloud Hopper — RDP arrival followed by schtasks persistence pointing at a writable directory
let WindowMin = 30m;
let RdpArrivals =
    DeviceLogonEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
    | where LogonType == "RemoteInteractive"        // Logon Type 10 — RDP
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
    | project LogonTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              ActorAccount = tolower(AccountName),
              SourceIP = RemoteIP, SourceHost = RemoteDeviceName,
              SourceIPType = RemoteIPType, LogonId;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create", "-create")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner RdpArrivals on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + WindowMin)
| where tolower(AccountName) == ActorAccount
| extend TaskTarget = extract(@"(?i)/tr\s+[""']?([^""']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine),
         TaskName   = extract(@"(?i)/tn\s+[""']?([^""']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine),
         RunAs      = extract(@"(?i)/ru\s+[""']?([^""']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| where TaskTarget has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Intel\")
      or TaskTarget has_any ("powershell","pwsh","rundll32","regsvr32","wscript","cscript","mshta","certutil","bitsadmin")
| extend MasqueradeTaskName = TaskName matches regex @"(?i)(google|update|chrome|adobe|windows|defender|onedrive|java|edge)"
| project LogonTime, TaskCreationTime = Timestamp,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, LogonTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          SourceIP, SourceIPType, SourceHost,
          TaskName, TaskTarget, RunAs, MasqueradeTaskName,
          ProcessCommandLine, ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by TaskCreationTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT10 / Cloud Hopper — RDP arrival followed by schtasks persistence to writable directory (Sentinel SecurityEvent)
let WindowMin = 30m;
let RdpArrivals =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4624 and LogonType == 10           // RDP success
    | where TargetUserName !endswith "$"
    | where TargetUserName !in~ ("system","local service","network service","anonymous logon")
    | project LogonTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              ActorAccount = tolower(TargetUserName),
              SourceIP = IpAddress, SourceWorkstation = WorkstationName,
              TargetLogonId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\schtasks.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any ("/create","-create")
| where Account !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner RdpArrivals on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (LogonTime .. LogonTime + WindowMin)
| where tolower(TargetUserName) == ActorAccount
| extend TaskTarget = extract(@"(?i)/tr\s+[""']?([^""']+)", 1, CommandLine),
         TaskName   = extract(@"(?i)/tn\s+[""']?([^""']+)", 1, CommandLine),
         RunAs      = extract(@"(?i)/ru\s+[""']?([^""']+)", 1, CommandLine)
| where TaskTarget has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\AppData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Intel\")
     or TaskTarget has_any ("powershell","pwsh","rundll32","regsvr32","wscript","cscript","mshta","certutil","bitsadmin")
| extend MasqueradeTaskName = TaskName matches regex @"(?i)(google|update|chrome|adobe|windows|defender|onedrive|java|edge)"
| project LogonTime, TaskCreationTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, LogonTime),
          Computer, Account = TargetUserName,
          SourceIP, SourceWorkstation,
          TaskName, TaskTarget, RunAs, MasqueradeTaskName,
          CommandLine, ParentProcess = ParentProcessName
| order by TaskCreationTime desc
ucs.rationale: Schtasks /create alone is noisy (legit installers, IT scripts). Pivoting purely on RDP is also noisy. APT10's Cloud Hopper signature is the SEQUENCE: trusted-relationship inbound RDP (often from an MSP jumpbox or partner subnet) → same-session schtasks /create → task target in a writable directory or invoking a script-host LOLBin → task name masquerading as Google/Adobe/Windows updater. PwC and BA
